prompt: The image file is not found when the application is packaged as a JAR. What is the bug?
choices: new File(path) uses the filesystem; inside a JAR, resources are not files — use getClass().getResourceAsStream() instead
File paths must be absolute when loading images
BufferedImage cannot be returned from a method
explanation: When packaged into a JAR, files inside the JAR are not accessible as File objects. File paths work during development but fail when deployed. Fix: use getClass().getResourceAsStream("/resources/logo.png") and pass the stream to ImageIO.read(stream). Resources are then loaded from the classpath regardless of packaging.
